perComponent.summary: The Vela Action Bar's floating pill (rounded-full white surface, filled blue circular count badge, medium-weight label, vertical action dividers, soft-danger destructive action, overlay shadow) matches the HeroUI Pro anatomy where comparable; two of three live shots are dominated by page chrome/docs so cannot be directly compared.

The one directly comparable rendered shot (Usage/week view) matches heroui.pro closely on layout, header, day-column badges, all-day bars, time grid and event styling; the other seven live shots are documentation prose/code rather than rendered components, so they are uncomparable.

Every local app-layout capture is a broken/empty render (uniformly near-black ~72/255 luminance, heavily blurred, ~15KB, no legible text/borders/color) versus the bright light-theme heroui.pro shots, so no variant can be honestly compared until the local screenshots are re-captured.

Vela's area-chart variants reproduce HeroUI Pro's area chart treatment (smooth blue line, soft light-blue/gradient fills, grid, axis ticks, stacked/multi tints, KPI inline sparkline) faithfully across all six sections.

Bar Chart matches HeroUI Pro on overall chart structure, axes, grid, rounded bar tops and blue color ramp, but local bars render noticeably wider than Pro's thin bars across single-series/horizontal variants, and the KPI-with-bar-chart trend indicator uses an oversized standalone green chevron instead of Pro's small inline up-arrow.

Carousel chrome (circular gray nav arrows, round dot indicators, slide gap, viewport layout) matches heroui.pro across variants; the only real signal is nav-arrow placement reading slightly more "inside the slide" than the live single-card gutter placement, plus a faintly pill-shaped active dot.

The cell color picker trigger (cell row, label, hex value, round swatch, border/radius/shadow) matches heroui.pro across all variants; the only real divergence is the Settings Group, which Vela renders as separate gapped pills instead of one connected grouped list.

Vela cell-select matches heroui.pro across all variants — rounded trigger, medium-weight label / muted value / up-down chevron, secondary grey-flat variant and disabled opacity all align; the only gaps are demo-composition choices (Font Family lacks the "Ag" font specimen, Settings Group mixes a secondary row), not component-styling defects.

Structure, track shape, label/value layout and typography all match heroui.pro, but every variant diverges on the core fill color: Vela paints the fill saturated primary blue while HeroUI Pro uses a subtle light "accent tint", and Vela's secondary/variants rows show a stray circular blue thumb instead of the thin pill indicator.

Every Vela cell-switch variant drops the leading text label and centers the switch inside the cell, whereas heroui.pro renders label-left / switch-right (justify-between); the pill container and switch control styling otherwise match.

Plain "Usage"/"Streaming" chain-of-thought variants match the live trigger+stepped-list anatomy, but both "Agent Trace" variants diverge structurally: the live reference composes multiple independent collapsible labeled sections (Thought / Generated design preview card / Explore nested file list / Found UI components) while Vela renders a flat steps list plus a single ChatTool card.

Vela's ChartTooltip card (rounded surface, muted xs header, dot/line indicators, semibold right-aligned values) closely matches heroui.pro across all variants; the only real divergence is the Inactive demo rendering a placeholder card instead of nothing.

Vela's chat-attachment preview chips, grouped row, and composer card closely match heroui.pro in shape, size, border and layout; only a couple of minor radius/state nuances and an icon/send-button-state difference (the latter being demo state).

Vela's chat-conversation matches heroui.pro across all three variants — centered message column, gray right-aligned user bubbles, left-aligned assistant text, bottom composer with blue send button, and the white circular scroll-to-bottom button all align in shape, color, radius, and spacing rhythm.

Vela's chat-list-view reproduces HeroUI Pro's two variants faithfully — multi-line rows (icon + bold title + preview + right-aligned timestamp) for Usage and single-line icon+title rows for Compact — with matching padding rhythm, typography scale, and structure; the only nuance is a slightly bluer persistent selection tint in the local demos where the live shots show neutral hover/no selection.

Vela chat-loader renders the same loader primitives (gray bouncing dots, blue pulse dot, blue spinner ring, and avatar+line skeleton) with matching size, shape, color semantics and radius as heroui.pro; the only difference is demo-harness label arrangement, which is out of scope.
Vela's chat-message-actions toolbar matches heroui.pro across all three variants: same horizontal ghost icon-button row, equivalent icon sizing, spacing rhythm, and muted neutral color treatment.

Vela chat-message matches the HeroUI Pro reference across user bubble, assistant avatar+body layout, typography, markdown rendering and spacing; the only deltas are demo-composition choices (absent default action row, Thinking label) rather than component/CSS parity gaps.

Single-chip variants (Usage/Document/Composable) match HeroUI Pro closely with only a minor added confidence badge, but the Grouped and Stacked Favicons variants diverge structurally: Vela renders flat inline chips instead of HeroUI's collapsible "N sources" / stacked-favicon disclosure.

Vela's ChatTool renders as a standalone bordered card with a right-side text-label-plus-colored-dot status and no disclosure chevron, whereas heroui.pro renders a chat-message bubble (AI avatar) with a leading status icon, a "<verb> tool: <name>" trigger label, and a trailing chevron disclosure that is collapsed by default.

Core card primitive (border, radius, selected primary ring, top-right indicator, disabled opacity) matches well across most variants, but icon-bearing variants use a centered icon-on-top layout instead of HeroUI's icon-leading horizontal tile, and the Grid Layout / Subscription Plans variants don't reproduce the multi-column grid and success-colored selected template.

Card surface, radius, header label and copy-button layout match heroui.pro closely; the only real gap is the syntax-highlight token palette, which uses a blue-keyword/green-string scheme versus the reference's pink-keyword/teal-function/orange-string scheme.

Every command variant shot shows only the closed trigger button (the modal palette dialog is unopened in both local and live), and on that visible surface the rounded trigger + kbd-badge styling matches HeroUI Pro; the only differences are demo-harness choices (trigger color/count, Chinese copy), not Command component parity gaps.

Vela's composed-chart renders the same bar+line / stacked / area+line / bar+area / multi-type geometries with matching blue palette, rounded bars, smooth lines and horizontal grid as heroui.pro; the only consistent visual gap is that the live demos wrap each chart in a titled card with a legend and a right-hand secondary Y-axis, whereas the local clean shots show the bare chart with a single left axis.

Across all seven variants both Vela and heroui.pro captured the ContextMenu in its closed/resting state, and the comparable trigger drop-zone box (dashed border, rounded corners, light fill, centered muted prompt) matches consistently; the open menu popover was not shown in any pair so the panel itself is uncomparable.

Core Data Grid chrome (header band, row borders/rhythm, status chips, checkbox selection, floating action bar, drag handles, pinned/resize affordances) matches heroui.pro closely, but several "showcase composition" variants (Editable Cells, Empty State, Users, Team Members, Servers) are reduced to a bare generic grid and omit the rich toolbars, inline edit controls, empty-state icon/CTA, sparklines/radial indicators and pagination that the live examples render.

The DropZone container variants (Usage/Disabled/Image Only/Max Size/Multiple/Custom Icon) match heroui.pro closely; the only real divergences are in the file-list cards, where Vela adds a left status accent stripe, omits the inline status check/X icon, and renders retry as plain text instead of a soft-danger pill.

Closed-trigger states match HeroUI Pro, but the open Inline picker inverts the search/grid/category stacking order vs the reference and the Sizes triggers don't show the sm/md/lg scale ramp; one local shot was a broken/empty capture.
Vela's emoji-reaction-button matches heroui.pro across all four variants — identical rounded-full pill shape, accent-blue selected treatment, dimmed disabled state, and proportional sm/md/lg scaling.

Empty State closely matches heroui.pro across structure, spacing, typography hierarchy, media circle, and size ramp; the only real signals are the avatar-group media rendering (non-overlapping avatars inside a grey blob vs an overlapping ring-bordered group) and a slightly decorative gradient on the with-background variant.

File Tree matches HeroUI Pro on the core component (chevron indicator, indentation, faint guide lines, rounded hover/selected row tint, checkbox/drag-handle/icon+label rhythm, and sm/md/lg size scaling); the only real gap is the Dynamic Collection demo rendering only its dataset-switcher with no tree body, plus a minor missing filter header in the PR review recipe.

The floating-toc bar-indicator trigger is a clean visual match to heroui.pro across all variants (gray bars, accent-blue wider active bar, right/left alignment, in-page pinning); the only differences are demo-content/harness choices (number of bars, trigger count, in-page placement side), not floating-toc.css gaps.

Vela's hover-card content panel (rounded overlay card, soft shadow, padding, enter/exit + placement animations) matches HeroUI Pro where comparable; the only recurring difference is the demo trigger element (Vela uses a solid primary pill, live uses inline text-links / outline buttons), which is harness/demo content rather than a hover-card styling gap.

The core inline-select trigger (ghost muted text + trailing double-chevron indicator, type scale and sizing) matches heroui.pro across all variants; the only real gap is the Team Switcher composition, which lacks the live demo's leading logo/slash and colored avatar-initial badge.

Core card-group structure, variant tints, grid layout, icon boxes and typography hierarchy match heroui.pro well; the consistent gap is that list/section/pressable variants omit the hairline row/header dividers the live uses, relying on whitespace gaps instead, and a few demos pick a different composition (card vs bare, missing trailing controls).

Item Card matches HeroUI Pro on card chrome, surface variants, icon box, and title/description hierarchy; the only flagged signals are a slightly heavier success-chip tint in Device List and demo-content interpretation differences (checkbox-cards/buttons vs live's inline select triggers).

Vela kanban matches HeroUI Pro on column-header treatment, card radius/shadow and overall multi-column layout, but its columns use a solid gray fill where the live default/project boards show transparent columns and the Notion board uses per-column color tints.

The KPI Group container (rounded surface card, equal-width horizontal columns / stacked vertical cells, hairline separators, label-above-value rhythm, shadow) matches heroui.pro across all three variants; the only visible gaps (trend delta pills, "from {previous}" secondary text) belong to the child KPI cell and the demo content, not the kpi-group container itself.

KPI layout, typography, icon-tint, progress and inline-chart structure track heroui.pro closely; the one repeated real divergence is the trend pill, which Vela renders as a solid saturated chip without a direction arrow vs the live soft-tinted pill with an up/down arrow.

Core line/dot/grid/axis rendering and the blue palette ramp match heroui.pro well; divergences are concentrated in composite demos (KPI, Stats, Portfolio) where the delta-indicator and card scaffolding differ, plus a curve-type difference on Traffic Source.

Vela's ListView reproduces HeroUI Pro's structure faithfully — gray-wrapped white card rows (primary) and transparent bottom-bordered rows (secondary), with matching radius, dividers, leading icons, checkbox column, two-line content, disabled greying and trailing actions; the only watch-item is a possible checked-checkbox shape difference.

Markdown rendering matches HeroUI Pro on layout, typography hierarchy, code-block container, blockquote and list styling; the one real gap is the local fenced code block lacking Shiki syntax-highlight token colors (renders flat monochrome).

Vela native-select matches heroui.pro across all 12 variants on control shape, radius, border, chevron, full-width, disabled, secondary-fill and variant treatment; the only real gaps are the invalid-state label not turning danger-red and a slightly darker/larger description+label typography.

Vela navbar matches HeroUI Pro on bar height, bottom-border chrome, brand/links/actions layout, dropdown caret, compact sizing, expanded-menu active-row highlight, and floating elevation; the only real gap is the Dashboard demo's simpler composition, and 6 live shots are doc/code-only so uncomparable.

The core Number Stepper control (pill group, circular white-surface +/- buttons, semibold tabular value, muted icons, label/description) matches heroui.pro closely across nearly all variants; only the Guest Picker layout and the With Custom Buttons color-variant showcase diverge, and those are demo-composition gaps rather than control-style breaks.

Vela NumberValue matches HeroUI Pro structurally and in formatting behavior across all variants; the only visible differences are demo-harness typography (live wraps the value in large/bold labeled-grid layouts vs Vela's bare inline render) which are out of scope, plus a low-severity question over prefix/suffix de-emphasis.

Vela pie-chart segment colors, center-content, nested-ring and breakdown layout match heroui.pro, but every local donut/pie shot was captured mid Recharts sweep animation so the arcs render only partially (90-300 deg) and the local clean-element shots lack the card/title/legend chrome the live examples show.

The pressable surface styling (button radius/border/padding/height, card rounding, disabled opacity) matches HeroUI Pro, but most local demos are minimal neutral-only placeholders that omit the live examples' full semantic color palette, captions, icon-button rows, and rich card/list-item structure.

Simple single-row variants (inline, compact, queue) match well, but the flagship composers (Usage, Secondary, Review Composer, With Suggestions, Run State) diverge structurally — Vela renders compact single-row pills where HeroUI Pro uses richer multi-zone composers (model-selector dropdown, action/diff pills, branch+progress toolbar, tall multi-line textarea, mic/add buttons, suggestion grid).

The default (pill) variant matches the live "Usage" example closely; the cards variant diverges because Vela renders borderless stacked-text items while HeroUI Pro wraps each card in a bordered, rounded card container.

Core radar rendering (polygon grid, angle/radius axis labels, blue series fill/stroke tiers) matches heroui.pro closely; the only real gaps are the Dots Only variant rendering nearly empty and the Comparison variant looking much smaller/flatter than the reference.

Core ring primitives (stroke width, rounded caps, blue palette, gray tracks) match well, but the Gauge sweep angle is 180° vs the live ~270°, and the Gauge Grid variant renders a single scaled gauge instead of the live 4-card grid of gauges with center percentages and semantic (red) color.

Card chrome (border, 2xl radius, blue selection ring, top-right radio indicator, custom check-circle, no-indicator, disabled dimming) matches HeroUI Pro closely, but Vela consistently center-aligns card title/description whereas Pro left-aligns content, and Pro's plan/payment cards lead with prominent price/badge typography that Vela's centered single-line layout omits.

The core Rating control (amber active / gray inactive star shape, size scale, disabled opacity, fractional partial fill, custom-icon and custom-color support) matches heroui.pro closely; the only divergences are in two composed showcase demos (Product Review and With Label) where Vela chose a different layout, which is largely demo content.

Vela's Resizable matches heroui.pro on layout, panel structure, 1px divider, rounded container, grip/pill indicator shapes and CSS-variable defaults; the only deltas are demo-harness choices (Vela demos opt into grip indicators on a few variants the live demo leaves plain) and several live shots that are docs/code rather than a rendered example.

Editor shell, content typography, blockquote, footer and disabled treatment closely track heroui.pro, but the local toolbar diverges in styling (filled grey bar + vertical group dividers + text-letter toggles vs live's borderless row of circular/icon buttons) and the Custom Composition variant keeps a top toolbar instead of relocating a compact toolbar into the footer.

Segment matches HeroUI Pro closely on its core treatments (grey-track + white shadowed indicator pill, ghost accent pill, sizes, disabled states); three demo variants (without-separators, theme-switcher, icon-expand) render with a different variant/pattern than the live reference, but the segment.css shard itself is correct.

Vela's Sheet trigger buttons match heroui.pro's flat/light primary-tinted pill styling across all 16 variants; no live screenshot shows an open sheet panel, so only closed-state triggers are comparable, and the few gaps are demo-harness scaffolding (missing toggle/status sub-elements) rather than component styling divergences.
Text Shimmer's default Usage variant matches heroui.pro's gradient treatment, typography, and card framing; the Color variant's live shot is page chrome only so it cannot be visually compared.

The core Timeline primitive (rail, status-tinted markers, connector, time/title/description typography) matches heroui.pro closely, but several Pro variants demonstrate richer slot compositions (nested content cards, stat tables, inline status tags, author/avatar meta, header groups, single-line compact rows, split meta-vs-content layout) that the Vela clean shots render only as a generic time/title/description (+ action buttons) timeline.

Vela's TrendChip renders the indicator icon as a large glyph stacked ABOVE the value text in a near-square block, whereas HeroUI Pro renders a compact horizontal inline pill badge (arrow + prefix + value + suffix on one line inside a small tinted, rounded background) — a fundamental structural divergence across every variant.

The Widget shell (rounded secondary-surface container, semibold title + muted trailing/legend header, white inset content with 1px ring + soft shadow, muted footer caption) matches heroui.pro closely across all variants; the only divergences are in the embedded chart sub-elements, which the Vela demos render as simplified axis-less sparklines.
